positive_global_styles: dark gospel blues soul with cinematic orchestral weight
voice-first production with cinematic and gospel influences
call and response between high pitch emotional male tenor and powerful dynamic female vocalist
harmony not unison on duet sections
male voice high pitch emotional tenor that soars and cracks with feeling
male vocals raspy with raw soul power and grit
female voice powerful dynamic soprano with range from whisper to belt like classic soul divas
cinematic builds from whisper to cathedral
staccato upper register piano motif throughout like amber pulses of light
epic and intimate at the same time
rap section in second verse with aggressive rhythmic delivery

positive_local_styles: staccato upper register piano notes scattered like flickering amber light
single female vocal hum enters warm and sustained
high pitch emotional male tenor voice vulnerable
powerful female voice answering from darkness
lines: A dead man wrote it down so we wouldn't forget
Two thousand years and the dust still hasn't settled

positive_local_styles: staccato upper piano notes flutter at phrase endings
high pitch emotional male tenor singing raw and aching
powerful dynamic female voice answering with force
voice first piano supports never leads
lines: We built this thing with splinters in our palms
No map no mercy
Just sawdust on the farm
And climbed the shelves to escape the fight
Watched it close in while we were trapped inside
And found our wisdom in the meditations
Started from the dust again
Blindfolded if we had to
Some of us don't talk about the fire we just swipe right by
Some lessons don't knock baby
They kick the door in
It ain't the fire it's who stayed when the air ran out
The only thing we ever really own
Is the love we keep fighting for

positive_local_styles: organ fades in underneath on last lines only
staccato amber motif steadies into rhythm
male and female voices rising together sung powerfully
lines: We can't tell the sky what to do with its rain
Can't hold back the flood
Or put these broken walls up again
No we never could
But we can lay every stone down where it matters most
That's the only thing that doesn't rot
The only kingdom that's complete

lines: We never know right from wrong but God will it hurt this long
Will it hurt this long
They'll crown anything that shines long enough
Bow to kings who turn to bones
Before the century has passed
Every single one of them
Every name they hammered into marble
Just someone who built a kingdom cold
And died reaching for a hand to hold

We chose being right over being here
And the house is cold when we get home
Every single time
That mountain they put in our path
Child that was the path
And the only life that dies too soon is the one no one touched

positive_local_styles: just low D pulse like heartbeat in dark room
male vocals sung softly with ache and vulnerability
piano drops out completely for one line creating total darkness
male voice gasps single breath coming back to air
piano re-enters higher register amber light returning

builds to three part harmony stack on turns out it was you
cinematic tension and release devastating and intimate
lines: Some of us lost our legs before we learned to stand
And still they told us run
We carry marks in places no one thinks to look
Wood and dark and shapes with no name
Felt the grain against our skin the dust of something built before us
The rhythm they left in our bones
We found the door by feel alone
Set the glass down watched the world steady itself
We were shaking not the room
We thought the kingdom was the walls we built
Turns out it was you
We spent our whole lives digging outward
When the well was always within
No house survives on one pair of hands
And when the walls fell in
Someone was still inside
Holding the weight
Amen
